name: Sample Player (Sliced)
description: This algorithm allows you to place a number of marker points on a sample, and conveniently trigger playback from the various marker points. It's your go-to algorithm for breakbeat slicing.
gui_description: The display shows the sample waveform with the slices indicated as check marks above. The last triggered slice is shown as the large number bottom right. Above this is the sample length (in seconds), the sample length in bars at the current tempo, and the calculated number of bars that the sample will loop over, if a clock is supplied and 'Auto trigger' is enabled.

specifications.name: Record time
specifications.description: The maximum length of audio that can be recorded (sampled).

parameters.name: Slice
parameters.description: Offsets the choice of which slice to play. Provided as a convenience when triggering playback with the 'Play' parameter.

parameters.name: Slices
parameters.description: Sets the number of slices in use.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.description: Sets the playback speed. Multiplied by the per-slice Speed parameter.

parameters.name: Transpose
parameters.description: Transposes the playback speed in semitones (across all slices).

parameters.name: Fine tune
parameters.description: Adjusts the playback speed in cents (across all slices).

parameters.name: Stop at slice end
parameters.description: If enabled, playback will stop when it reaches the next slice. Otherwise, playback continues to the end of the sample.

parameters.name: Detect transients
parameters.description: When this parameter changes from 0 to 1, it invokes the 'Detect transients' function exactly as if the user had selected it from the menu.

parameters.name: Auto trigger
parameters.description: Sets whether the sample will automatically be triggered by the clock. This can be at a fixed interval, or set automatically by the length of the loop.

parameters.name: Auto divisor
parameters.description: Sets how often the sample will be triggered, if 'Auto trigger' is 'Fixed'.

parameters.name: Time sig denom
parameters.description: The time signature denominator: one of 1, 2, 4, 8, or 16.

parameters.name: Coarse
parameters.description: The coarse slice position, as a percentage of the sample length.

parameters.name: Fine
parameters.description: The fine slice position, in sample frames.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.description: The playback speed for the slice.

input_ports.name: Slice input
input_ports.description: CV input for slice selection (V/oct, scaled at one semitone per slice).

input_ports.name: Trigger input
input_ports.description: Trigger or gate input for triggering slices.

input_ports.name: Advance input
input_ports.description: Trigger input to advance to the next slice.

input_ports.name: Reset input
input_ports.description: Trigger input to reset to slice 1.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: Clock input (24 ppqn) for automatic loop playback.

input_ports.name: Slice input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the slice CV input (V/oct, scaled at one semitone per slice).

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the clock input (24 ppqn).

input_ports.name: Run/stop input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the run/stop input.

input_ports.name: Advance input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the advance input (to step through the slices).

input_ports.name: Reset input
input_ports.description: The bus to use for the reset input (to reset to slice 1).

output_ports.name: Audio output
output_ports.description: Audio output for the sliced sample playback.

output_ports.name: Slice CV
output_ports.description: CV output indicating the current slice selection.
